idea.hook: The Universe is 13.8 Billion Years Old.
facts: Light travels at a finite speed (approx. 300,000 km/s).
So, the maximum distance light could travel to us since the Big Bang is 13.8 billion light-years.
You'd expect our 'observable universe' to be 27.6 billion light-years across (diameter).
idea.payoff: But due to the universe's constant expansion, the objects at the edge of our observable universe are actually an astounding 93 BILLION light-years away from us!
idea.title: 🤯 The Universe is WAY BIGGER Than You Think!

scenes.text: The Universe is 13.8 Billion Years Old.
scenes.visual: A serene deep space view with faint, ancient galaxies shimmering in the distance.

scenes.text: Light has a speed limit: 300,000 km/s.
scenes.visual: A bright white light beam streaks horizontally across a dark, starry background, emphasizing speed.

scenes.text: Max light travel distance: 13.8 BILLION light-years.
scenes.visual: Earth (small) in the center. A line extends from Earth to a distant point, labeled '13.8 BILLION LY'.

scenes.text: Expected observable universe: 27.6 BILLION LY diameter.
scenes.visual: A circular diagram with Earth at its center, showing a diameter labeled '27.6 BILLION LY' across distant galaxies.

scenes.text: BUT due to expansion, it's 93 BILLION light-years across!
scenes.visual: The previous circular diagram dramatically expands outwards, the '27.6 BILLION LY' label morphing into '93 BILLION LY' with an energetic, swirling nebula background.

youtube.title: 🤯 The Universe is WAY BIGGER Than You Think!
